prs.title: feat(export): append Markdrop watermark to exported markdown files
prs.body:
Closes #4

## Description

This PR implements the custom watermark feature for markdown exports. When users export their markdown files, a watermark is automatically appended at the end of the document with a link to the Markdrop repository.

## Type of Change

- [x] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)

## Changes Made

- Added WATERMARK constant containing attribution text and repository link
- Modified blocksToMarkdown() function to append watermark to exported content
- Updated export toast message to indicate watermark has been added
- Cleaned up code formatting and removed unused imports

## Checklist

- [x] My code follows the project's code style
- [x] I have performed a self-review of my code
- [x] I have tested my changes locally
- [x] My changes generate no new warnings

prs.title: Integrate SonarQube for code quality analysis
prs.body:
## Description

This PR integrates SonarQube for automated code quality analysis into the project.

## Changes Made

1. **Added sonar-project.properties** - Configured Java 21 as the source and target version for SonarQube analysis. This configuration file specifies project identification, source code locations, and encoding settings.

2. **Created GitHub Actions workflow** - Added `.github/workflows/sonarqube.yml` which automates SonarQube analysis on pushes to main and sonarqube-integration branches, as well as on pull requests. The workflow includes:
   - Checkout of code with full history
   - Java 21 setup with Temurin distribution
   - Build and test execution with Maven
   - SonarQube scan using SonarCloud
   - Quality Gate check with 5-minute timeout

## Related Issue

Closes #17

prs.title: Enable custom properties
prs.body:
## Changes

- Added `DbvStarterProperties` configuration class with `@ConfigurationProperties` to support custom properties
- Integrated properties into `DatabaseValidationService`
- Added configuration flags:
  - `dbv.springboot.starter.verify-tables` (default: `true`)
  - `dbv.springboot.starter.verify-views` (default: `false`)

Resolves Fixes #16

## Benefits

- Enables flexible configuration for table and view verification
- Maintains backward compatibility with default settings
- Provides better control over validation behavior

prs.title: Enable custom properties
prs.body:
This PR adds support for custom properties configuration to enable flexible table and view verification.

## Changes
- Added `DbvStarterProperties` configuration class with `@ConfigurationProperties` to support custom property configuration via `application.properties` or `application.yml`
- Implemented two configurable boolean properties:
  - `dbv.springboot.starter.verify-tables` (default: `true`) - Controls whether to verify table existence
  - `dbv.springboot.starter.verify-views` (default: `false`) - Controls whether to verify view existence
- Updated `DatabaseValidationService` to use these properties when validating database objects
- Modified `DBVAutoConfiguration` to inject `DbvStarterProperties` into the service

## Resolves
Fixes #16

## Benefits
- Users can now selectively enable/disable table and view verification based on their needs
- Provides more flexibility in database validation scenarios
- Maintains backward compatibility with default settings

prs.title: Add hello_world.algol to print 'HELLO WORLD!'
prs.body: This PR adds a Hello World program in ALGOL language as requested in issue #25. The program prints "Hello World" to demonstrate basic ALGOL syntax.

prs.title: Aurora css scene
prs.body:
Closes #486

This PR adds a pure CSS Aurora Borealis (Northern Lights) animated scene featuring:
- 5 layered gradient waves with smooth, continuous animations
- Realistic color palette (green, cyan, blue, purple, pink)
- Starry night sky with twinkling effect
- Mountain and forest silhouettes for depth
- Glowing animated title
- Fully responsive design
- Pure CSS implementation with no JavaScript

All animations use different durations (18-30s) and delays for natural, non-repetitive motion that mimics real aurora borealis behavior.

prs.title: Add GitHub Actions workflow for automated release builds
prs.body:
Hi @Omkumar2003! 

This PR addresses issue #27 by adding an automated GitHub Actions workflow that builds release assets on every push to the main branch.

## What this PR does:

 Creates `.github/workflows/release.yml` that automatically:
  - Builds the standalone `.exe` using `pkg` (maintains ~38MB size)
  - Zips the Chrome extension from `LeetCode-Token-Viewer` folder
  - Uploads both as downloadable artifacts

## Technical Details:

Uses Node.js 18 (matching your `node18-win-x64` pkg target)
Exact build command: `pkg ok.js --targets node18-win-x64 --output LeetcodeAutomater.exe`
Extension zip: Creates `LeetcodeAutomater-extension.zip` from the `LeetCode-Token-Viewer` folder
Trigger: Runs on every push to `main` branch

## Workflow Highlights:

-  No changes to existing code - only adds workflow file
-  Respects all your build preferences (pkg, Node 18, ~38MB exe)
-  Uses official GitHub Actions (`actions/checkout@v3`, `actions/setup-node@v4`, `actions/upload-artifact@v4`)
- Clean, maintainable YAML structure
Testing:

Once merged, you can test by:
1. Pushing any commit to `main`
2. Going to Actions tab → "Build and Release Assets" workflow
3. Downloading the artifacts from the completed run

## Future Enhancements (Optional):

If you'd like, I can add:
- Automatic attachment to GitHub Releases
- Version tagging support
- Multi-platform builds (Linux, macOS)

Fixes #27

Let me know if you'd like any changes!

prs.title: Add new ML/DL resources for issue #445
prs.body:
## Description

Hello! 👋

This PR addresses issue #445 by adding comprehensive ML/DL resources to the ML/README.md file.

## Changes Made

- ✅ Added extensive resources for **Large Language Models (LLMs)** including:
  - Hugging Face NLP Course
  - Stanford CS224N: Natural Language Processing with Deep Learning
  - LLM University by Cohere
  - Fine-tuning guides from DeepLearning.AI
  - OpenAI GPT best practices

- ✅ Added resources for **Transformers Architecture** including:
  - Illustrated Transformer by Jay Alammar
  - Original "Attention Is All You Need" paper
  - The Annotated Transformer
  - Hugging Face Transformers Documentation
  - Stanford CS25: Transformers United

- ✅ Added resources for **Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs)** including:
  - GAN Lab - Interactive Visualization
  - Stanford CS236: Deep Generative Models
  - GAN Specialization by DeepLearning.AI
  - PyTorch GAN Tutorial
  - Papers with Code - GANs

- ✅ Added resources for **Neural Network Architectures** including:
  - Deep Learning Architectures Visual Guide
  - ResNet, EfficientNet, Vision Transformers papers
  - CNN Explainer - Interactive Visualization

- ✅ Added **Additional Resources** section with:
  - Papers with Code
  - Arxiv Sanity Preserver
  - Distill.pub for visual ML explanations
  - Two Minute Papers YouTube channel

## Formatting Improvements

- Fixed HTML comment formatting
- Standardized badge color notation (COLOR → color)
- Cleaned up README structure for better readability

All resources are high-quality, beginner-friendly yet comprehensive, and personally curated for students looking to learn these advanced ML/DL topics.

## Issue Reference

Closes #445

Thank you for reviewing this PR! I'm happy to make any changes if needed. 🙏

prs.title: Implement circle geometry toolkit with visualization
prs.body:
This pull request introduces a comprehensive Circle Geometry Toolkit implemented in Python. The code defines a Circle class capable of:

Calculating the area and circumference of a circle.

Checking intersection between two circles with varying radii.

Providing a clear string representation for debugging and display.

Visualizing two circles graphically using matplotlib for better geometric intuition.

This toolkit combines mathematical precision with visual feedback to facilitate geometric operations involving circles, which could be used in graphics projects, simulations, or educational tools.

The code is structured for clarity, reusability, and testing, enhancing the codebase with robust geometric functionality.

prs.title: Add intersection check for circles with different radii
prs.body:
Added a function to check if two circles with different radii intersect.

Details:

Calculates the distance between centers.

Compares this distance to the sum and difference of the radii to determine intersection.

Includes tests for touching, overlapping, and non-intersecting circles.

Reason:
This helps handle geometric problems or features that deal with circles of varying sizes.
